danger_signals.visual_form: Reanimated creature aesthetics; darkened/desaturated sprite palette
danger_signals.meaning: Areas under enemy control or magical corruption; heightened threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Amphitheater/enclosed castle chambers with dramatic lighting
danger_signals.meaning: Significant power confrontation; mandatory combat checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Glowing magical stones with elemental color coding (Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Time, Heaven)
danger_signals.meaning: Political/power struggle nodes; world-state tied to Crest possession

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive demonic warrior design, recurring boss encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Phalanx's authority enforcer; respect-based threat rather than pure antagonism

safety_signals.visual_form: Consumable spell vellums, flasks, talismans in accessible locations
Crest collection triggers visual/mechanical form changes
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; expanded safe exploration options via new abilities
Player agency; non-linear progression safety valve

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fire breath abilities; claw climbing mechanics associated with protagonist progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crest of Heaven; possession of five primary Crests
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transforms into hideous beast form when wielding Crest of Infinity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle throne room; centralizes Crest collection; establishes anarchy control when defeated

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Recurring boss enemy; demonic warrior aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple confrontations with gradually increased difficulty
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Patrol/guardian role across game stages; reluctantly returns Crests to Firebrand out of respect

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Amphitheater arena; forced combat against Somulo (zombified Demon Dragon)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player begins with limited transformation capability; must prove strength through combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crests scattered across accessible regions; castle dominion established
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arma blocks progression paths; Firebrand must regain Crests to access new areas (Metroidvania gating)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Elemental transformation forms unlock; Ground Gargoyle, Aerial Gargoyle, Tidal Gargoyle, Legendary Gargoyle appearance changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New movement/combat abilities enable previously inaccessible stage areas; power visualization tied to progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crest of Infinity summon; Phalanx beast transformation or sealing outcomes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player choice (destructive vs. containment vs. restraint) determines ending cinematic and unlocks Ultimate Gargoyle form

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Phalanx death without containment; realm falls into chaos
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Least favorable outcome; visual/narrative consequence of aggressive victory

environmental_storytelling.example: NPC dialogue (unused text) reveals wall-surrounded shop unavailable to players; suggests oppressive environmental control preventing resource access
Six playable areas tied to six Crest elements (Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Time, Heaven); visual environment palette shifts reflect elemental dominion
Arma encounters in stage progression; Phalanx in castle; spatial hierarchy communicates authority structure
Firebrand visual form changes mirror mechanical empowerment and story advancement; each transformation unlocks new exploration capability
JP version uses 'ZAM' currency renamed to 'G.P.' (Gold Points) in NA; regional text changes (Blazon→Crest Power) signal faction/power naming conventions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Forced initial opponent; represents corrupted power under Phalanx control; doubled health in NA version vs. JP (difficulty signal)
Repeatedly confronts Firebrand; gradually returns Crests due to respect for Firebrand's strength; indicates faction schism (respect over absolute loyalty)
Remains in castle; summons Crest of Infinity for final transformation; three behavioral outcomes based on player action choice
Gains new transformation forms with each Crest collected; mechanical ability expansion signals narrative agency and rising threat to Phalanx authority

notes: Demon's Crest uses elemental Crest collection as primary world-state mechanism. The game's visual communication relies on: (1) transformation form as power indicator; (2) Crest-locked area access (Metroidvania gating); (3) boss positioning as faction authority markers; (4) color/palette shifts for elemental environments. Regional differences (JP 'Blazon'→NA 'Crest', currency ZAM→G.P., enemy health scaling) suggest localization affecting faction/power naming conventions. The unused spell shop dialogue indicates environmental oppression as world-state signal. Multiple endings tied to player choice (Phalanx death, sealing, or transformation outcome) drive branching narrative manifestation. Anti-piracy mechanisms (pause menu lockout, enemy invincibility on detected piracy) are meta-signals rather than diegetic world-state, but indicate design intent to protect narrative experience.
